title: Cardio — Analysis
text:
Show cohort filters
echo_cohort <- master %>%
  filter(
    echo_ef_notavailable___999      == 0 |
    echo_late_notavailable___999    == 0 |
    echo_gls_notavailable___999     == 0 |
    echo_earatio_notavailable___999 == 0 |
    echo_eeratio_notavailable___999 == 0
  ) %>%
  # Harmonize echo_gls sign convention: some centers store negatives (the
  # standard convention), others store the absolute value. Take abs() once
  # here so every downstream summary (medians, abnormal flag, spline) uses
  # magnitudes only.
  mutate(echo_gls = abs(echo_gls))

preterli_echo_base <- echo_cohort %>%
  # Pre-terli OR same-day echo (per analyst spec — adds 13 same-day patients
  # on top of the strict pre-terli set).
  filter(days_to_echo <= days_to_terli_initiation_day0)

# --- Youden-optimal echo_ef cutoff (Task 9 input) --------------------------
# Compute once on the cohort so the same cutoff is used to (a) derive the
# echo_ef_abnormal_youden flag below and (b) render Task 9.
.youden_data <- preterli_echo_base %>%
  filter(!is.na(echo_ef), !is.na(hrs_responders_cat_2))
.roc_ef <- pROC::roc(.youden_data$hrs_responders_cat_2, .youden_data$echo_ef,
                     quiet = TRUE, direction = "auto")
.youden_coords <- pROC::coords(.roc_ef, x = "best", best.method = "youden",
                               ret = c("threshold","sensitivity",
                                       "specificity","accuracy","ppv","npv"))
youden_ef_cutoff <- as.numeric(.youden_coords$threshold)

preterli_echo <- preterli_echo_base %>%
  mutate(
    # Abnormal-echo flags (1 = abnormal, 0 = normal, NA if measurement missing)
    echo_ef_abnormal        = if_else(is.na(echo_ef),
                                      NA_real_, as.numeric(echo_ef < 60)),
    echo_ef_abnormal_youden = if_else(is.na(echo_ef),
                                      NA_real_, as.numeric(echo_ef < youden_ef_cutoff)),
    echo_eeratio_abnormal   = if_else(is.na(echo_eeratio),
                                      NA_real_, as.numeric(echo_eeratio > 15)),
    # echo_gls is already stored as a magnitude (see harmonization in echo_cohort).
    echo_gls_abnormal       = if_else(is.na(echo_gls),
                                      NA_real_, as.numeric(echo_gls < 18)),
    echo_lateral_e_abnormal = if_else(is.na(echo_lateral_e),
                                      NA_real_, as.numeric(echo_lateral_e < 10)),
    # TR is unit-aware: < 100 treated as m/s (cutoff 2.8); >= 100 as cm/s (cutoff 280).
    echo_tr_abnormal        = if_else(is.na(echo_tr),
                                      NA_real_,
                                      as.numeric(if_else(echo_tr < 100,
                                                         echo_tr > 2.8,
                                                         echo_tr > 280))),
    echo_lavi_abnormal      = if_else(is.na(echo_lavi),
                                      NA_real_, as.numeric(echo_lavi > 34))
  )

cat("Echo cohort N      :", nrow(echo_cohort),    "\n")


Echo cohort N      : 243 


Show cohort filters
cat("Pre-terli cohort N :", nrow(preterli_echo), "\n")


Pre-terli cohort N : 107

title: Cardio — Analysis
text:
8.1 echo_ef — Pre-terli cohort
Cohort: Pre-terli + same-day echo (N = 107)


Show spline fit + plot
res_8_1 <- spline_logit(preterli_echo, "echo_ef",
                        xlab = "Ejection fraction (%)",
                        cohort_label = "Pre-terli cohort")
summary(res_8_1$fit)



Call:
glm(formula = .y ~ ns(.x, df = df), family = binomial, data = d)

Coefficients:
                 Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)
(Intercept)        0.3697     1.2031   0.307    0.759
ns(.x, df = df)1  -1.2823     0.9092  -1.410    0.158
ns(.x, df = df)2   0.9588     2.6934   0.356    0.722
ns(.x, df = df)3  -0.2668     1.0862  -0.246    0.806

(Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)

    Null deviance: 141.45  on 106  degrees of freedom
Residual deviance: 137.83  on 103  degrees of freedom
AIC: 145.83

Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4


Show spline fit + plot
res_8_1$plot

title: Cardio — Analysis
text:
8.2 echo_ef — Echo cohort
Cohort: Echo cohort (N = 243)

res_8_2 <- spline_logit(echo_cohort, "echo_ef",
                        xlab = "Ejection fraction (%)",
                        cohort_label = "Echo cohort (all echos)")
summary(res_8_2$fit)

Coefficients:

ns(.x, df = df)1  -0.9152     0.6718  -1.362    0.173
ns(.x, df = df)2   0.1227     2.0877   0.059    0.953
ns(.x, df = df)3  -0.6916     1.1161  -0.620    0.535

title: Derived Variables
text: All variables computed in Tables.Rmd (and replicated in revision 0311.Rmd) from the REDCap source data.

title: Derived Variables
text:
Identifiers & Filtering








Variable
Description
Logic




id
Internal row ID per center
paste0("{center}_", row_number())


Discont_Day
Day terlipressin discontinued (0–13)
First day where discont_terli_dayN == 1 via qc_check()


center_id
Center abbreviation
str_extract(subjectid, "^[^-]+")


center
Numeric center grouping (factor, 1–10)
BID/MGH/Yale=1, PEN=2, OCH=3, BSW=4, CPM/CSF/UNM=5, Mayo/MCM/NWM=7, MMC=9, CCF/INU=10

title: Derived Variables
text:
Baseline Creatinine & eGFR








Variable
Description
Logic




baseline_scr_calculated
Estimated SCr from eGFR=75
Inverse CKD-EPI 2021 via numerical optimization (optimize())


baseline_scr_new
Best available baseline SCr
(1) baseline_scr if days_to_admit != 0, else (2) baseline_scr_120to365_value, else (3) min(calculated, scr_admit)


baseline_scr_new_2
Strict baseline (no calculated fallback)
Only options (1) and (2) above


eGFR
Estimated GFR
CKD-EPI 2021 from baseline_scr_new_2


ckd_baseline
Baseline CKD
eGFR < 60 → 1

title: Derived Variables
text:
AKI Staging








Variable
Description
Logic




ratio
SCr ratio at terli initiation
scr_terli_day0 / baseline_scr_new


delta
SCr delta at terli initiation
scr_terli_day0 - baseline_scr_new


akin
AKIN stage at initiation
1: ratio 1.5–2 OR delta > 0.3; 2: ratio 2–3; 3: ratio ≥ 3 OR SCr ≥ 4


discont_ratio
SCr ratio at discontinuation
lowest_creatinine / baseline_scr_new


discont_delta
SCr delta at discontinuation
lowest_creatinine - baseline_scr_new


discont_aki_stage
AKIN stage at discontinuation
Same staging as akin, using lowest_creatinine


difference
AKI stage change
akin - discont_aki_stage

title: Derived Variables
text:
Clinical Scores

Child-Pugh Score



Variable
Description




child_pugh_score
Sum of 5 components (5–15)









NoteComponent scoring








Component
1 point
2 points
3 points




Bilirubin (mg/dL)
< 2
2–3
> 3


Albumin (g/dL)
> 3.5
2.8–3.5
< 2.8


INR
< 1.7
1.7–2.3
> 2.3


Ascites
Absent (1)
Slight (2)
Moderate (3)


HE grade
None (0)
Grade 1–2
Grade 3–4








CLIF-C Score







Variable
Description




clif_c_score
10 * (0.33 * organ_score + 0.04 * age + 0.63 * log(WBC) - 2)


clif_c_score_new
Median-imputed (missing → population median)









NoteOrgan sub-scores














Organ
1 point
2 points
3 points




Liver (T.bili mg/dL)
< 6
6–12
≥ 12


Kidney (SCr mg/dL)
< 2
2–3.5
≥ 3.5


Brain (HE grade)
1
2–3
4–5


Coagulation (INR)
< 2
2–2.5
≥ 2.5


Circulation
MAP ≥ 70, no pressors
MAP < 70, no pressors
Any pressors


Respiratory (SpO2/FiO2)
> 357
214–357
≤ 214








ACLF Grade & Organ Failures








Variable
Description
Logic




aclf_grade
Organ failure count (capped at 3)
Count of organ failures below


organ_failure_sum
Total organ failure count (uncapped)
Sum of 6 binary flags


liver_f
Liver failure
bili_terli_day0 >= 6


brain_f
Brain failure
he_terli_day0 > 1


coagulation_f
Coagulation failure
inr_terli_day0 >= 2


circulatory_f
Circulatory failure
map_terli_day0_time0 < 70


respiratory_f
Respiratory failure
spo2_terli_day0 / fio2_terli_day0 <= 357


kidney_f
Kidney failure
scr_terli_day0 >= 1.5





MELD Scores








Variable
Description
Formula




day0_meld
MELD at Day 0
9.57*log(Cr) + 3.78*log(bili) + 11.20*log(INR) + 6.43; Cr capped 1–4


day0_meld_na
MELD-Na at Day 0
MELD + sodium correction (Na capped 125–137); applied only if MELD > 11


day0_meld_3
MELD 3.0 at Day 0
Includes sex factor (+1.33 if female), albumin, sodium, and bilirubin-sodium / albumin-Cr interactions


admit_meld
MELD at admission
Same as day0_meld using admission labs


admit_meld_na
MELD-Na at admission



admit_meld_3
MELD 3.0 at admission
Median-imputed if missing


admit_meld_3_raw
MELD 3.0 raw (pre-imputation)

title: Derived Variables
text:
Outcome Variables

HRS Response








Variable
Description
Logic




lowest_creatinine
Lowest SCr around discontinuation
min(last_scr_2, last_scr, scr_day1_post_terli)


last_scr
SCr on discontinuation day
scr_terli_day{Discont_Day}


last_scr_2
SCr day before discontinuation
scr_terli_day{Discont_Day - 1}


HRS_reversal_new
Primary outcome
lowest_creatinine <= 1.5 AND (scr_day0 - lowest_creatinine) >= 0.3


hrs_responders
Response category
0 = No stage improvement, 1 = Partial (improved but SCr > 0.3 above baseline), 2 = Complete (≤ 0.3 above baseline)


hrs_responders_cat_2
Binary responder
0 = Non-responder, 1 = Any response


hrs_rebound
SCr rebound post-terli
scr_day1_post_terli - last_scr >= 0.3





Survival / Competing Risk

Intermediate Variables








Variable
Description
Logic




dc_death_status
In-hospital death
1 if disposition == 5, else 0


after_discharge_90days_death_status
Died within 90 days post-discharge
1 if dc_death_90days == 1, else 0


time_to_death
Days from terlipressin initiation to death or censoring
In-hospital death: days_to_discharge; post-discharge death: days_to_discharge + dc_days_to_death; lost to follow-up: days_to_discharge + days_to_lost_to_followup; alive at 90 days: days_to_discharge + 90 (intentional censoring). All minus days_to_terli_initiation_day0


time_to_death_status
Overall death indicator
1 = died (in-hospital or post-discharge within 90 days), 0 = alive or lost to follow-up





90-Day Death Outcomes









Variable
Description
Values
Logic




time_to_death_90days
Follow-up time for 90-day death analysis (days)
0–90
min(time_to_death, 90) – all follow-up capped at 90 days


time_to_death_status_90days
90-day death event indicator
1 = died within 90 days; 0 = alive at 90 days, OR died after 90 days (censored at 90), OR lost to follow-up
1 only if time_to_death_status == 1 AND time_to_death <= 90; all other patients get 0





90-Day Transplant Outcomes









Variable
Description
Values
Logic




time_to_transplant_90days
Follow-up time for 90-day transplant analysis (days)
0–90
min(time_to_transplant_cmp, 90)


time_to_transplant_status_90days
90-day transplant event indicator
2 = transplanted within 90 days; 0 = not transplanted within 90 days, OR transplanted after 90 days (censored at 90)
2 only if transplant_lt_status == 2 AND time_to_transplant_cmp <= 90; all other patients get 0





90-Day Competing Risk (Death vs. Transplant)









Variable
Description
Values
Logic




time_to_death_90days_cmp
Competing risk follow-up time (days)
0–90
min(time_to_death_90days, time_to_transplant_90days) – whichever event came first


time_to_death_status_90days_cmp
Competing risk event indicator
0 = censored (alive, no transplant within 90 days); 1 = death within 90 days (death came first or tied); 2 = transplant within 90 days (transplant came first)
If death time ≤ transplant time: use death status; if transplant time < death time: use transplant status









NoteHow the competing risk status works






Patient dies day 30, no transplant: time = 30, status = 1 (death)
Patient transplanted day 20, alive at 90 days: time = 20, status = 2 (transplant)
Patient alive at 90 days, no transplant: time = 90, status = 0 (censored)
Patient dies day 50, transplanted day 40: time = 40, status = 2 (transplant came first)
Patient died after 90 days: time = 90, status = 0 (censored – death beyond window)
Patient lost to follow-up day 60: time = 60, status = 0 (censored)







Transplant








Variable
Description
Logic




transplant_lt
LT timing category
1 = During hospitalization (LT or SLKT), 2 = Within 90 days post-discharge, 3 = After 90 days (within 1 year), 0 = No transplant


transplant_slkt
Simultaneous liver-kidney transplant indicator
1 if slkt_during_hospital == 1 OR dc_lt_90days == 2 OR lt_type_1yr == 2, else 0


transplant_lt_status
Transplant status for competing risk
0 = no transplant, 2 = transplanted (coded as 2 for competing risk with death = 1)


time_to_transplant
Days from terlipressin initiation to transplant
NA if no transplant


time_to_transplant_cmp
Transplant time for competing risk (999 if no transplant)
999 serves as a large censoring time so pmin with death time selects death


transplant_outcome
Transplanted within 90 days (binary)
1 if time_to_transplant_status_90days == 2, else 0


listed_transplant
Listed for liver transplant
1 if lt_eval == 2 OR lteval_listingstatus == 1, else 0

100 * (scr_admit * ua_na) / (sodium_admit * ua_cr)

title: Derived Variables
text:
Multiple Imputation (revision 0311)
Predictive mean matching (PMM), m=10 imputations, 50 iterations, seed=12345.
Imputed predictors: sex_male, scr_admit, tbili_admit, sodium_admit, inr_admit, albumin_admit, bili_terli_day0, he_terli_day0, inr_terli_day0, map_terli_day0_time0, fio2_terli_day0, spo2_terli_day0, scr_terli_day0, wbc_terli_day0
Post-imputation: Clinical scores (CLIF-C, ACLF grade, Child-Pugh, MELD) are recomputed on each imputed dataset.
Models fit: Fine-Gray competing risk models pooled with Rubin’s rules via run_pooled_fg(), stratified by HRS criteria, transplant listing status, and other subgroups.

title: REDCap Variable List
text: Complete variable list from the Terlipressin Database REDCap Code Book (PID: 43500). The database contains 18 instruments with 1500+ fields. Treatment day instruments (Day 0–13) share identical structure and are documented once below.

title: REDCap Variable List
text:
Terlipressin Treatment Days 0–13
Each treatment day (Day 0 through Day 13) contains identical fields. Variables are suffixed _day0 through _day13. Below uses N as placeholder.

Terlipressin Treatment









Variable Pattern
Field Label
Type
Values




terli_date_dayN
Day N date
date



days_to_terli_initiation_dayN
Days from admission to day N of terlipressin
calculated



discont_terli_dayN
Was terlipressin discontinued?
binary
1 = Yes, 0 = No


discont_reason_terli_dayN
What was the reason of discontinuation?
categorical
1 = Adverse Effect (abdominal pain, nausea, diarrhea, volume overload, bradycardia), 2 = Achieved HRS Reversal (serum creatinine < or = 1.5 mg/dL), 3 = Worsening Kidney Function or Requiring Renal Replacement Therapy, 4 = Death, 5 = Transitioned to Hospice, 6 = Further Decompensation Requiring ICU care, 7 = Respiratory Failure, 8 = Ischemic Related Event (skin discoloration, cyanosis, organ ischemia), 9 = Other

Field Label
Type
Values

title: Cardio — Project Summary
text: The Cardio sub-project characterizes the cardiopulmonary (echocardiographic) phenotype of HRS-AKI patients in the HARMONY Terlipressin Registry — focused on echos performed before terlipressin initiation.

title: Cardio — Project Summary
text: The Cardio sub-project characterizes the cardiopulmonary (echocardiographic) phenotype of HRS-AKI patients in the HARMONY Terlipressin Registry — focused on echos performed before terlipressin initiation.

title: Cardio — Project Summary
text:
Cohorts
Echo cohort — at least one of these REDCap availability flags is 0 (measurement present):

echo_ef_notavailable___999 — Ejection fraction
echo_late_notavailable___999 — Lateral e′ velocity
echo_gls_notavailable___999 — Global longitudinal strain
echo_earatio_notavailable___999 — Mitral E/A ratio
echo_eeratio_notavailable___999 — E/e′ ratio

Pre-terli echo cohort — echo cohort AND days_to_echo < days_to_terli_initiation_day0. All Table 1s and the spline model use this cohort.

title: Cardio — Project Summary
text:
Outputs









#
Output
Stratifier
Notes




1–3

title: Cardio — Project Summary
text:
Pipeline





flowchart TD
    A[final_master_01282026.xlsx<br/>main Terlipressin project] --> B[Source shared_pipeline.qmd]
    B --> C[Echo cohort<br/>any non-NA echo availability flag]
    C --> D{Echo timing<br/>vs days_to_terli_initiation_day0}
    D -- before --> E[Pre-terli echo cohort]
    D -- same day --> F[Counted only]
    D -- after --> G[Counted only]
    E --> H[Tasks 4–7<br/>stratified Table 1s]
    E --> I[Task 8<br/>EF cubic-spline LR]
